title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Claddock and Wetherell review investigation reports concerning Alfred. A taxi driver, prompted by a memory of a horse named Clover winning a race, recalls a passenger matching the victim's description traveling to Paddington on the day of the incident. Wetherell presents a report noting that Detective Inspector Ricky observed Alfred dining with Oddie Evans, an associate of Dicky Rogers, at a restaurant called "Real" on Friday, December 20th, at 9:30 p.m. Additionally, a ticket inspector at Blachampton Station identified Alfred. Claddock concludes that Alfred had sufficient time to commit the murder at Lathford Manor and return to London via the 11:55 p.m. train.

At Lathford Manor, the Crickenshaw family holds a tense dinner. Harold blames Emma for involving Scotland Yard, while Cedric defends her, arguing that keeping Matilda's disappearance a secret would only increase suspicion. Alfred and Harold exchange insults regarding their respective alibis. Dr. Campbell intervenes to support Emma's decision to seek professional advice. Meanwhile, Lucy prepares a curry for the household, including for the demanding Old Creaky-Soup.

After leaving the manor in a fury, Dr. Campbell returns home at 3:00 a.m. following the birth of twins to Mr. and Mrs. Simpson. He is awakened by a 3:00 a.m. phone call from Lucy, who reports that everyone at the manor has become sick. After providing emergency instructions, Campbell arrives at the manor. Three hours later, a fatigued Campbell and Lucy sit in the kitchen. Campbell notes that while the family's condition has stabilized, he intends to investigate the cause of the illness and determine who prepared the dinner.
